title: Changes to attach to enable per-stream attaching
body:
This allows us to attach to attach to just stdout or stderr or stdin, or any combination of these.

This needs tests, and is having terminal issues, but the code should be complete.

comments.body: Test aren't happy with this mheon.
Pushed a fix for terminal attach, which seems to be working fine now. I think I managed to break non-terminal attach, though.

Getting closer. Apparently you can't compare interfaces like io.Reader to nil and expect a good result, so I had to restructure things a bit to determine which streams were attaching. Bash in a non-terminal container still not working right, though.
Nevermind that "not working right" bit - it works exactly the same as docker, so I think it's good enough. Let me right some tests and we should be golden.
Test failures are unrelated - we're leaking open files somewhere in the tests
Ginkgo itself seems to be leaking containers/storage lock FDs
@rhatdan @baude @TomSweeneyRedHat @umohnani8 PTAL, let's get this merged and cut a release

:pushpin: Commit 55b6f4f has been approved by `baude`

:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
